basics.name: Jesús Vellojin
basics.label: Assistant Professor — Numerical Analysis & Scientific Computing
basics.summary: My research is distinguished by a strong computational focus, emphasizing efficiency and robustness as fundamental pillars. I develop innovative computational tools for solving and validating mathematical models using a range of numerical methods, promoting the advancement of practical applications in the mathematical and computational sciences.

work.name: Departamento de Ciencias, Universidad Técnica Federico Santa María
work.summary: Full-time faculty position in the Department of Sciences.

work.name: Universidad del Bío-Bío
work.summary: Mathematical and computational analysis of solid and fluid mechanics.
highlights: Numerical implementation of elastic and solid vibration models.
Finite element methods for fluid-structure interactions and viscoelastic slender structures.

work.name: Universidad del Bío-Bío
highlights: Numerical implementation of non-linear coupled fluid-transport problems.
Finite element analysis of the reverse osmosis process.

courses: Thesis: Análisis Numérico de Estructuras Viscoelásticas Delgadas
Thesis: Análisis e Implementación de Métodos de Descomposición de Dominios para un problema de Stokes
Advisor: Dr. rer. nat. Ricardo Prato Torres
Thesis: Homología y Homotopías en Retículos Finitos

projects.name: Nuevos desarrollos en la aplicación del método de elementos virtuales a flujos en medios porosos
projects.entity: Universidad Militar Nueva Granada, Bogotá, Colombia

projects.name: Research Stay — Università Milano-Bicocca
projects.summary: New computational tools for the implementation of the virtual element method in 2D and 3D for the simulation of fluid and solid mechanics problems.

projects.name: Research Stay — Monash University
projects.summary: Construction and analysis of new discretisations for coupled flow-transport problems relevant in desalination processes.

projects.name: Further development in numerical methods for thin structures and applications — FONDECYT 1181098
projects.entity: Universidad Técnica Federico Santa María, Chile
projects.summary: PhD-student researcher (2018–2020); technical staff and programmer (2020–2022). Analysis of thin structures through numerical methods.

projects.name: Coupling of finite and boundary elements using the Nitsche technique
projects.summary: MATLAB programmer: codes to solve an optimal control problem in elasticity using the Nitsche technique.

languages.fluency: Advanced — fluent in writing, reading and conversation
